claim: AlphaFoldExtractor subclasses BatchedFeatureExtractor to fetch AlphaFold-DB predicted structures (AF-{UNIPROT_ID}-F1-model_v4.pdb) and extract 9 structural descriptors per Syn3A CDS: mean pLDDT, std pLDDT, disorder fraction (pLDDT<50), helix / sheet / coil composition via Ramachandran torsion bins, sequence length, radius of gyration, and a has_structure boolean. biopython is imported lazily inside _ensure_loaded. Missing structures (404 from AFDB, unmapped UniProt IDs, parse failures) produce an NaN row with has_structure=0.0 rather than raising. The cache parquet is not populated by this session; the Colab notebook fetches all structures and writes cell_sim/features/cache/alphafold_db.parquet.
source_detail: Session 14. Verifiable by reading the module + running 6 sandbox-safe tests. Structures themselves come from the AlphaFold Protein Structure Database (EBI-EMBL) — Varadi et al. 2024 Nucleic Acids Research — fetched on demand by the extractor at run time. The AFDB structures are NOT registered as a first-class memory_bank source; they are external read-only data that the extractor pulls via HTTP.

context.commit_range: TBD — filled in once Session 14 push lands.

caveats: Cache parquet is empty until the Colab notebook runs. Until then the feature block is NaN for every locus_tag.

Secondary-structure classification is a torsion-bin proxy for DSSP (the AlphaFold-DB does not ship DSSP data, and DSSP itself is optional in typical Colab envs). Helix / sheet / coil fractions should be treated as ~10-15% noisier than DSSP; they are ordinal features, not precise ones. A follow-up session can wire in the full DSSP parser if warranted by downstream MCC.
AFDB coverage of Syn3A UniProt IDs is expected to be ~80-90%; rows without coverage are NaN-filled with has_structure=0.0.
No MCC measurement is claimed. A follow-up measured fact will document any detector built on top of these features.
Network is required to populate; the extractor retries 3x with backoff on transient failures and records 404s as missing-structure (permanent, no retry).
